gpu_note: ~16GB VRAM. Models up to ~14GB (Q4-class) are treated as full GPU fit. Larger weights spill to system RAM.

deepseek-r1:14b.best_for: Best reasoning/VRAM ratio — chain-of-thought without spilling to RAM

deepseek-r1:1.5b.best_for: Tiny reasoning model for quick experiments and low-latency checks

deepseek-r1:8b.best_for: Strong reasoning in a compact footprint

devstral-small-2:24b.best_for: Agentic coding — explore codebases, edit multiple files, tool use (384K context)

gemma3:27b.best_for: General purpose, larger Gemma 3 (spills to RAM)

gemma4:12b.best_for: Gemma 4 all-rounder — reasoning, coding, vision, and agent workflows

gemma4:26b-a4b-it-qat.best_for: Gemma 4 MoE near-30B quality, QAT-tuned for 16GB cards — keep context short

gpt-oss:120b.best_for: Largest open gpt-oss model — batch quality over interactive speed (heavy RAM spill)

gpt-oss:20b.best_for: Fastest interactive gpt-oss on 16GB — high tokens/sec, fully on GPU

llama3.1:8b.best_for: General purpose Meta Llama 3.1 — fast and reliable

lfm2.5:8b.best_for: Edge MoE tuned for fast, reliable tool calling on consumer GPUs

ministral-3:14b.best_for: Mistral edge model — vision + tools, strong speed on 16GB

mistral-small3.2:24b.best_for: Mistral Small 3.2 — better instruction following, function calling, and vision

phi4:14b.best_for: Math, STEM, and coding reasoning (Microsoft Phi-4)

qwen3:14b.best_for: Qwen3 with thinking mode — strong instruction following

qwen3-coder:30b.best_for: Coding with large context (spills to RAM)

qwen3-vl:8b.best_for: Qwen3 vision-language — stronger multimodal than Llama 3.2 Vision

qwen3.5:9b.best_for: Qwen 3.5 multimodal with thinking — best general upgrade in the ~7GB class

qwen3.5:9b-q8_0.best_for: Higher-quality Qwen 3.5 9B (Q8) — still fully on GPU

qwen3.5:27b.best_for: Strong Qwen 3.5 quality — tight on 16GB; keep context short
